zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead於1612年11月29日出生在英國肯特騰特爾登時，她的父親Stephen Mead22歲，母親Sarah Henley20歲。她於1621年與Captain William John Collins Lewis在英國英格蘭素可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1657年5月12日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead于1612年11月29日出生在英国肯特騰特爾登时，她的父亲Stephen Mead22岁，母亲Sarah Henley20岁。她于1621年与Captain William John Collins Lewis在英国英格兰素可结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1657年5月12日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gunther, Heinz, Horst, Kurt.
German: variant of   Stolz  .

variants.name: Stolz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Heinz, Erwin, Gunter, Hans, Helmut, Helmuth, Mathias, Reinhard, Seigfried.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Middle High German and German stolz ‘magnificent, proud’, Yiddish shtolts ‘proud’.

nameMeanings.name: Mead
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a meadow, from Middle English mede ‘meadow’ (Old English mǣd), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places so named, such as Meads in Harford (Devon).
perhaps from Middle English mede ‘mead, alcoholic drink of fermented honey and water’, possibly denoting one who was fond of the drink or who made or sold mead. Compare Meader .

variants.name: Meadow
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a meadow, from Middle English medewe, medu (Old English mǣdwe, dative of mǣd). Compare Mead 1.

variants.name: Meads
meanings: English: variant of Mead , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Meade
meanings: Irish: habitational name from Midheach, the Gaelic name of County Meath in Ireland. Its Anglicized forms are now indistinguishable from those of the English name Mead .
English: variant of   Mead  .

variants.name: Mather
meanings: English:
occupational name for a mower or reaper of grass or hay, Old English mǣthere. Compare Mead , Mower . Hay was formerly of great importance, not only as feed for animals in winter but also for bedding.
variant of Mader , reflecting an alternative pronunciation influenced by Old Norse mathra ‘madder (plant)’.

variants.name: Meader
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English meder ‘maker or seller of mead’. Compare Mead 2.

variants.name: Medd
meanings: variant of   Mead   with shortening of the vowel.
possibly from Middle English Medde, a rare personal name of obscure origin.
English:

variants.name: Meddaugh
meanings: Perhaps Irish, related to Midheach (see Mead ).

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Hanhah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead was born on 29 November 1612, in Tenterden, Kent, England, her father, Stephen Mead, was 22 and her mother, Sarah Henley, was 20. She married Captain William John Collins Lewis in 1621, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 12 May 1657, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 44.

Elle a épousé Captain William John Collins Lewis en 1621, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 12 mai 1657, à Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 44 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead(이)가 1612년 11월 29일 영국 켄트 텐터든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Stephen Mead(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Henley(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1621년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 Captain William John Collins Lewis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1657년 5월 12일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead родилась 29 ноября 1612 г. в местности тентирден, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Stephen Mead было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Sarah Henley, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Captain William John Collins Lewis в 1621 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 12 мая 1657 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 44 лет.
